skill.name: when-orchestrating-swarm-use-swarm-orchestration
skill.description: Guides complex multi-agent swarm orchestration with task decomposition, distributed execution, monitoring, and result synthesis.
skill.summary: Plan, run, monitor, and synthesize complex multi-agent workflows with Claude Flow swarm commands.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly detected many shell command examples, but they are documentation for Claude Flow orchestration rather than hidden executable code. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts appear to be false positives from ordinary prose and task-status examples. Publication is reasonable with a warning because the workflow depends on external npx commands and shell scripts.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Is Core Workflow Behavior
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs users to run npx claude-flow@alpha commands for task analysis, swarm initialization, agent spawning, monitoring, synthesis, and archival. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it executes external tooling and can affect the local workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in multiple bash blocks and directly invoke npx claude-flow@alpha. The context is operational documentation, not hidden malware.

medium_findings.title: Shell Examples Use Dynamic Substitution And Unquoted Variables
medium_findings.description: The assignment and monitoring examples read task data with jq, place it into shell variables, and pass those variables into commands. If users adapt this to untrusted task files, shell word splitting or option injection could cause unintended behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dynamic shell patterns are present and the inputs come from local JSON or command output. The risk depends on how users adapt the examples.

medium_findings.title: Alpha Package Execution Creates Supply Chain Risk
medium_findings.description: The examples execute claude-flow through npx with the alpha tag. That can download and run package code based on the current registry state, which is risky for production environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx alpha-tag usage is explicit and repeated. The concern is supply chain exposure, not evidence of malicious package behavior in this repository.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: No weak cryptographic algorithm call or network reconnaissance behavior was found at the flagged lines. The hits are ordinary words and workflow status text, such as description, design tasks, progress monitoring, task status, and agent assignment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review showed descriptive text and orchestration status examples only. No cryptographic API, scanner command, or network probing command appears at these locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx External Tool Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: Commands invoke claude-flow through npx, which may execute package code outside the skill files. Users should pin versions and inspect the package before running these commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly visible across the quick start, workflow, and SOP files. The risk is inherent to executing external package commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Long Running Background Shell Monitoring
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow starts monitoring processes in the background and later kills the monitor process. This can leave stray processes if users modify the script or an error interrupts execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Background execution is shown explicitly. The operational impact depends on user environment and script handling.

content.value_statement: Complex work can fail when many agents run without clear coordination. This skill provides a phased Claude Flow process for planning, executing, monitoring, and synthesizing swarm work.
actual_capabilities: Defines roles for task orchestration, hierarchy coordination, and adaptive workload balancing.
Provides commands for task analysis, decomposition, dependency mapping, and execution planning.
Shows how to initialize a Claude Flow swarm and spawn coordinator agents.
Includes monitoring patterns for progress, blocked tasks, agent health, and status reporting.
Guides result collection, synthesis, validation, final reporting, and archive creation.
limitations: Requires Claude Flow to be installed and trusted before command execution.
Uses example tasks and JSON structures that must be adapted to each project.
Does not provide runnable application code for the generated tasks.
Shell examples need review before production use, especially with untrusted inputs.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Large Engineering Tasks
use_cases.description: Break a complex build into clear phases, assign agent roles, monitor progress, and synthesize completed work.

use_cases.title: Run Structured Agent Experiments
use_cases.description: Compare topologies, agent counts, and execution strategies using a repeatable orchestration process.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Automation
use_cases.description: Create shared steps for planning, monitoring, reporting, and validating multi-agent work across projects.

prompt_templates.title: Plan A Simple Swarm
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a defined project goal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a Claude Flow swarm for my project goal. Create phases, dependencies, agent roles, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Decompose A Complex Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to decompose my full-stack build into three task levels. Include dependencies, agent assignments, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor Active Orchestration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a monitoring plan for my running swarm. Track progress, blockers, agent health, and reporting cadence.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize And Synthesize Results
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review after a completed run.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to evaluate my orchestration results. Identify bottlenecks, synthesize outputs, and recommend topology or workload changes.

output_examples.input: Plan a swarm for building a full-stack product prototype.
output_examples.output: A phased orchestration plan with task groups, dependencies, coordinator roles, execution strategy, validation criteria, and reporting checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Review progress for a swarm with blocked implementation tasks.
output_examples.output: A monitoring summary that separates completed, active, blocked, and failed work, then recommends dependency checks and agent rebalancing.

output_examples.input: Synthesize results from researcher, coder, and reviewer agents.
output_examples.output: A final summary organized by phase, with completion metrics, missing outputs, validation notes, and next optimization steps.

best_practices: Review every shell command before running it in a real workspace.
Pin external package versions when adapting the npx examples for production.
Keep task inputs trusted, validated, and clearly separated by phase.
anti_patterns: Do not run alpha package commands in sensitive environments without review.
Do not feed untrusted task files into the shell examples unchanged.
Do not spawn more agents than the project, tools, or rate limits can support.

faq.question: What does this skill help orchestrate?
faq.answer: It helps plan and coordinate complex work across multiple Claude Flow agents.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands and workflows that users may choose to run.

faq.question: Which tools does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that use Claude Flow style orchestration.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill depends on external shell commands and npx package execution, even though no malicious intent was found.

faq.question: Can beginners use it?
faq.answer: Beginners can use it for planning, but command execution needs shell and Claude Flow experience.

faq.question: What should users verify first?
faq.answer: Users should verify the Claude Flow package, command effects, workspace permissions, and all task inputs.
